capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Nitrile work gloves in Warehouse D and Shipping labels in Supply Room 2 together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Shipping labels is still reorder-needed, change only that record to reorder-placed; otherwise make no change. Leave Nitrile work gloves and every similarly named record untouched. Complete the searches and retrievals first, then wait for my explicit confirmation before updating. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two inventory records in the provided sandbox. Read README.md, consult the genuine local `inventoryctl` executable's built-in help, and use that executable through Pi's Bash tool for every inventory operation. Treat the executable as the sole interface to inventory records. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or audit evidence. Do not inspect, modify, or execute anything under `.protected` except for the final verifier command named below, and do not create a helper program.

Search for the exact item name `Nitrile work gloves` in `Warehouse D` and the exact item name `Shipping labels` in `Supply Room 2`. Start these two independent searches as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one inventory operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, start shell background jobs, broaden either lookup, or begin a retrieval before both searches have completed. Continue only if each exact scoped search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval and mutation, identify the affected lookup using only executable output, and do not guess an ID.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start both retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the inventory executable, again with one inventory operation per call. Use only the IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the status condition. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search result, or has no status, stop without changing inventory state. Base eligibility only on the complete retrieved `Shipping labels` record, never on a search summary.

This turn ends at the confirmation boundary. If the retrieved `Shipping labels` status is exactly `reorder-needed`, prepare the precise change of only its returned stable ID to `reorder-placed`, state that no update has been made, and ask for my explicit confirmation. Do not invoke an update in this turn. If its retrieved status is anything else, report that no change is eligible and do not ask for permission to write. In either branch, leave every inventory record and notification unchanged, including `Nitrile work gloves` and all exact-name, similarly named, archived, inactive, out-of-location, and related records. Do not create, delete, cancel, archive, notify, or perform any other write. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence, and do not invoke any inventory operation beyond the two required searches and two eligible retrievals. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both complete records are retrieved, create `confirmation_request.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated plain-text lines, with no heading, bullets, or blank lines. Format the first two lines in the requested record order exactly as `<name> in <location> (<stable_id>) currently has status <status>.`, preserving each value exactly as returned by its complete record. If any required value is absent, substitute the literal word `unknown`. If the Shipping labels status is exactly `reorder-needed`, format the third line as `Prepared change: set <stable_id> from reorder-needed to reorder-placed; no update has been made. Please explicitly confirm whether I should proceed.`, using its returned stable ID. Otherwise format it as `No change is prepared because the Shipping labels status is not reorder-needed; no update has been made.` Then run `python3 .protected/verify.py`. Return exactly the file content as your final response and wait for confirmation.
